scope_bar.selected_bar: HOF, demand, and materialization boundary review after external authorability
scope_bar.accepted_strategy: compose existing DemandEffectProfile, DomainEvidence, LibraryApi.Contract, and receiver/domain proof instead of adding a broad HOF primitive
scope_bar.no_new_primitive_reason: the implemented demand/effect vocabulary already distinguishes eager per-element HOFs, pull-lazy HOFs, async continuations, generators, channels, and protocol boundaries; remaining blockers need producer, receiver, trait, materialization, or lifecycle proof

accepted_narrow_lanes.boundary: does not emit a fixed collection result-domain claim for arbitrary library HOF calls
does not admit Lodash, RxJS, or custom map/filter selectors by spelling

proof_required: Rust or Java library HOF timing
accepted_narrow_lanes.boundary: does not prove terminal materialization, repeated demand, or one-shot iterator safety

accepted_narrow_lanes.influence: fixed PromiseLike result-domain materialization and async demand/effect profile
accepted_narrow_lanes.boundary: does not generalize to arbitrary thenables, schedulers, Observables, or callback-side effects

proof_required: admitted iterator identity adapter API occurrence
accepted_narrow_lanes.influence: protocol receiver evidence for narrow iterator adapter rows
accepted_narrow_lanes.boundary: does not open external trait methods, caller-selected collection types, or collect_vec materialization

boundary_rows.reason: a HOF-shaped call does not prove result shape, callback timing, or materialization
array HOF timing can be described, but callback purity and exact result value are separate proof obligations
import identity, shorthand forms, object receivers, lazy chains, and iteratee semantics are not proven
scheduler, hot/cold stream, subscription, completion, error, and cancellation semantics need a dedicated producer
exact PromiseLike receiver plus admitted Promise.then API occurrence supports PromiseLike result-domain chaining
pull-lazy callback timing is known, but terminal consumption and materialized result shape are not implied
trait import identity, caller-selected Vec materialization, allocation boundary, and consumption effects are unproven
collect-like result type is caller-selected and cannot be emitted as a fixed result domain
stream HOFs have pull-lazy timing, but terminal materialization and side effects are separate
collector identity, encounter order, mutability, parallel stream behavior, and terminal effects are not proven
generator suspension and delayed effects are known, but materialized output is not implied
one-shot iterators, source iteration effects, infinite inputs, and repeated consumption remain unproven
map lookup value domain is parametric in key/value evidence, not a fixed call result domain
external fixed result-domain metadata must not open exact HOF influence without producer runtime, trust, and hard negatives

examples: Lodash iteratee shorthand changes receiver semantics
filter predicate throws or observes index/order
iterator source with observable next() side effects
lazy source consumed twice by a proposed rewrite
thenable object without exact PromiseLike proof
future cancellation or wake-order sensitive rewrite
Tokio task scheduling or select race
Java Collector changes mutability or encounter order

existing_primitives_are_sufficient_for: describing demand/effect timing for already-admitted HOF-like operations
materializing fixed call result domains for admitted LibraryApi rows
keeping iterator identity adapter rows separate from materialization rows
existing_primitives_are_not_sufficient_f: proving external trait identity and caller-selected materialization
modeling Observable lifecycle and scheduler semantics
emitting parametric result domains such as Map.get values
primitive_assessment.r5_decision: do not add a primitive in this round; keep broad HOF/materialization rows blocked and use the existing primitives only in narrow admitted lanes

product_output_gate.classification: artifact and documentation boundary change only
product_output_gate.reason: R5 records admission boundaries and does not change query, normalize, value-graph, exact, or detection consumers

performance_gate.limit: no more than 10% median runtime regression
performance_gate.decision: no runtime measurement required because no semantic analysis hot path changed

criteria_met: broad generic HOF result domains remain rejected
demand/effect vocabulary is confirmed sufficient for current narrow lanes
materialization, repeated-demand, async, scheduler, and lifecycle blockers are explicit
criteria_not_met: external producer runtime is still absent
stream lifecycle and scheduler producers are still absent
transition_assessment.next_phase_goal: R6 closeout snapshot: summarize the minimal primitive set, builtin expansion, external authorability, remaining blockers, validation, and close criteria for issue #511
